mission.title: Install and Configure KubeEdge on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeEdge is a Kubernetes Native Edge Computing Framework that extends container orchestration and device management capabilities to edge hosts. By installing KubeEdge, you can manage edge applications and devices seamlessly using Kubernetes APIs, enhancing data processing and privacy at the edge.

steps.title: Install keadm CLI
steps.description:
Download the KubeEdge admin tool:
```bash
export KUBEEDGE_VERSION=v1.23.0
curl -Lo keadm.tar.gz https://github.com/kubeedge/kubeedge/releases/download/${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}/keadm-${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}-linux-amd64.tar.gz
tar xzf keadm.tar.gz
sudo mv keadm-${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}-linux-amd64/keadm/keadm /usr/local/bin/
rm -rf keadm.tar.gz keadm-${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}-linux-amd64
```

steps.title: Initialize the cloud side (control plane)
steps.description:
On the cloud node (where kubectl has access):
```bash
keadm init --advertise-address=<cloud-node-ip> --kubeedge-version=${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}
```
This installs CloudCore on the cloud node.

steps.title: Join edge nodes
steps.description:
Get the token from the cloud node:
```bash
keadm gettoken
```
On each edge node, join the cluster:
```bash
keadm join --cloudcore-ipport=<cloud-node-ip>:10000 --token=<token> --kubeedge-version=${KUBEEDGE_VERSION}
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get nodes
keadm version
```
Edge nodes should appear in the node list.

uninstall.title: Remove edge nodes
uninstall.description:
On each edge node:
```bash
keadm reset
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeEdge
upgrade.description:
```bash
keadm upgrade cloud --kubeedge-version=v1.23.0
# On edge nodes:
keadm upgrade edge --kubeedge-version=v1.23.0
```

troubleshooting.title: Edge node not connecting
troubleshooting.description:
Check EdgeCore logs on the edge node:
```bash
journalctl -u edgecore -f
```

troubleshooting.title: CloudCore not starting
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
journalctl -u cloudcore -f
```
Verify the advertise address is reachable from edge nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods not scheduling on edge
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the edge node is Ready:
```bash
kubectl get nodes
kubectl describe node <edge-node>
```

resolution.summary: KubeEdge is installed with CloudCore on the cloud side and EdgeCore on edge nodes.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster is required for the cloud side. Edge nodes need network access to the cloud node on port 10000.
